title: Larry Vs Michael J Fox

jokes.text: Greg innocently draws Hitler with mustache and asks Larry what a 'Hitler' is
jokes.setup: Child drawing Nazi imagery without understanding the context
jokes.punchline: Larry has to explain Hitler to a 7-year-old
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry getting into uncomfortable territory through innocent circumstances. Strong character work.

jokes.text: Greg says he wants a swastika for his birthday and they should sell them in NYC gift shops
jokes.setup: Child likes the geometric pattern of the swastika
jokes.punchline: Wants one as a gift and thinks they should be sold commercially
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the uncomfortable situation. Child's innocent logic makes it even more cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: Greg tells Jews to 'Get a life!' when Larry explains they wouldn't like swastika gift shops
jokes.setup: Larry explains Jews wouldn't like swastika gift shops
jokes.punchline: Child dismissively tells entire Jewish population to get a life
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy. The casual dismissal from a child creates maximum discomfort in perfect Curb fashion.

jokes.text: Larry shushes people talking during background piano music at a bar
jokes.setup: Woman playing background piano music in a bar
jokes.punchline: Larry treating casual bar music like Carnegie Hall
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness, perfectly in character but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: It's not Carnegie Hall. It's just a bar.
jokes.setup: Michael J. Fox shushes Larry for talking during piano
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical distinction between performance venues
jokes.explanation: Good observational point that sets up the central conflict. The economy is strong.

jokes.text: Fox! Fox! Fox!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get Michael's attention across the bar
jokes.punchline: Loudly yelling during the piano performance he was just shushed for
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Larry immediately does exactly what he was told not to do, but louder.

jokes.text: Larry awkwardly tries to describe Greg as 'flamboyant' to his mother
jokes.setup: Larry struggling to diplomatically discuss Greg's behavior
jokes.punchline: Settles on 'flamboyant' as his tactful description
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry awkwardness around sensitive topics, though somewhat routine.

jokes.text: You know, challenging to have a son like that. You know, his being so... Flamboyant.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing Greg with his mother
jokes.punchline: His awkward assumption about the child's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry putting his foot in his mouth about sensitive topics with strangers.

jokes.text: Larry offers to buy Greg a gift 'if it helps with the sex' and Diane confirms it will
jokes.setup: Larry offering to buy child a birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Transactional view of gift-giving for sexual favors
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry cynicism about relationships being transactional. Perfectly crafted exchange.

jokes.text: Larry asks 'Was it pissed or Parkinson's?' about Michael's head shake
jokes.setup: Michael J. Fox shook his head at Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry can't tell if it was anger or neurological condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant premise that sets up the entire episode conflict. Perfectly economical and quotable.

jokes.text: What a terrible inconvenience. My God, I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about Michael hitting multiple elevator buttons
jokes.punchline: Michael's sarcastic overreaction to Larry's impatience
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic escalation that shows both characters' pettiness.

jokes.text: Larry and Michael's escalating elevator button fight
jokes.setup: Michael hits two elevator buttons, Larry complains
jokes.punchline: Escalates to them hitting every floor button out of spite
jokes.explanation: Great escalation sequence showing two petty adults acting like children.

jokes.text: Why don't I go to 43? I'll go to 40. I'll go to 42.
jokes.setup: Elevator argument about button pressing
jokes.punchline: Both men childishly pressing every button in spite
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy showing two grown men acting like children over elevator etiquette.

jokes.text: Michael says 'I'm a head-shaking fool. I always shake' about his Parkinson's
jokes.setup: Larry asking if the head shake was anger or Parkinson's
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact description of his condition
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor from Michael's character about his condition.

jokes.text: Where are you gonna see one of my movies? It was like 1985 last time I was in a movie.
jokes.setup: Larry's analogy about talking during Michael's movies
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-deprecating reference to his career decline
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about his reduced acting career, well-integrated with the character.

jokes.text: Michael says 'I'll be back in two shakes' before leaving
jokes.setup: Michael excusing himself from conversation
jokes.punchline: Uses shaking pun given his Parkinson's condition
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but somewhat obvious given the setup. Character-specific though.

jokes.text: Larry draws Hitler mustache on magazine cover of Susie's father-in-law
jokes.setup: Larry doodling Hitler mustaches as established earlier
jokes.punchline: Accidentally defaces respected businessman on magazine cover
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the Hitler mustache running gag into more serious territory.

jokes.text: Michael shakes up soda can due to Parkinson's and it explodes on Larry
jokes.setup: Michael offering Larry a soda
jokes.punchline: His shaking causes the can to explode when opened
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy that plays into the episode's central premise about Parkinson's vs. intentional actions.

jokes.text: Leon's elaborate theory about Michael handing Larry his dick and it shooting sperm
jokes.setup: Leon explaining everything Michael touches gets shaken up
jokes.punchline: Extends logic to increasingly absurd sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Pure Leon absurdity that takes the premise to ridiculous extremes. Classic character work.

jokes.text: Greg gets excited about making Gone with the Wind and Wizard of Oz costumes with sewing machine
jokes.setup: Greg receives sewing machine as birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans elaborate costume projects, confirming stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Confirms everyone's suspicions about the appropriateness of the gift choice.

jokes.text: Susie asks 'What, are you trying to turn him gay?' about the sewing machine
jokes.setup: Larry gave effeminate child a sewing machine
jokes.punchline: Susie directly states what everyone's thinking
jokes.explanation: Susie's blunt reaction to Larry's obliviousness. Predictable but in character.

jokes.text: Larry says 'I think he might be gay' and 'He's pre-gay'
jokes.setup: Defending his gift choice for the child
jokes.punchline: Coins the term 'pre-gay' for a seven-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great Larry logic and coinable phrase. Perfectly captures his awkward reasoning.

jokes.text: Leon says 'Michael J. Fox about to be Michael J. Fucked-up in a minute'
jokes.setup: Leon threatening to confront Michael J. Fox about noise
jokes.punchline: Transforms actor's name into threat
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon wordplay that's both threatening and ridiculous. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That shaking shit might come in handy. I don't fuckin' know. Right? You don't know this motherfucker's skills.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining why Larry vs Michael would be a fair fight
jokes.punchline: Leon's theory that Parkinson's might be a fighting advantage
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon absurdist logic, perfectly in character. The idea of Parkinson's as a secret weapon is hilariously wrong.

jokes.text: Leon says Larry vs Michael is 'even Steven' because of the shaking
jokes.setup: Larry worried about fighting Michael J. Fox
jokes.punchline: Leon thinks Parkinson's levels the playing field
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical Leon reasoning about disability as fighting equalizer.

jokes.text: I think you're upset about the shushing. I think you were pissed off about the Hitler mustache on your father-in-law. I think you shook up that can of soda on purpose.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Michael about the noise complaints
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid theory connecting all events into deliberate harassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Larry's paranoia, tying together all the episode's conflicts into one conspiracy theory.

jokes.text: You're handling balls. So it's gay.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests sports equipment as gifts
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous logic about any ball-handling being sexual
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry overthinking that leads to absurd conclusions. The logic is flawed but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Of course I'd take a bullet for you. What, are you kidding? Without a doubt.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Jeff would take a bullet for him
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Setup for the later payoff. Jeff's quick agreement contrasts with what actually happens.

jokes.text: People call Jeff 'poor bastard' when he leaves with Larry
jokes.setup: Larry asking Jeff if he'd take a bullet for him
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jeff's reputation as Larry's long-suffering friend
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about their friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: Michael bumps Larry and says 'that wasn't Parkinson's, that was just bad timing'
jokes.setup: Larry asking if bump was Parkinson's related
jokes.punchline: Michael admits this one actually was intentional
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that validates Larry's paranoia. Great comedic timing.

jokes.text: Larry says Michael is using Parkinson's to 'take advantage of the non-Parkinson's'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his theory to building management
jokes.punchline: Creates demographic divide between Parkinson's and non-Parkinson's people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic creating sociological theory from personal grievance.

jokes.text: You think Mr. Parkinson, the first guy who had Parkinson's-- You think he would like what he's seeing here?
jokes.setup: Larry defending himself to the building board
jokes.punchline: Inventing a fictional 'Mr. Parkinson' as moral authority
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist logic. Creating a fictional eponym and appealing to his theoretical moral standards is peak Larry.

jokes.text: Greg makes swastika pillow sham and credits Larry for teaching him about Hitler
jokes.setup: Greg proudly presents handmade gift to Susie
jokes.punchline: It's a swastika and he explains Larry taught him about it
jokes.explanation: Masterful payoff to the Hitler subplot. The child's innocent pride makes it even worse.

jokes.text: Jeff gets hit by bike and says 'I took a bullet for you' to Susie
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion about taking bullets for loved ones
jokes.punchline: Jeff literally takes impact to protect Susie, though it's just a bike
jokes.explanation: Good callback with physical comedy payoff. Jeff proving his devotion literally.

jokes.text: Larry makes violin playing gesture and crowd thinks he's mocking Michael
jokes.setup: Larry trying to silently communicate about violin gift
jokes.punchline: Hand gesture looks like mocking Parkinson's tremors
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual miscommunication that escalates Larry's situation to public humiliation.

jokes.text: This guy is trying to put Parkinson's out of business and I'm here to put you out of this city.
jokes.setup: Mayor Bloomberg defending Michael J. Fox
jokes.punchline: Treating Larry as threat to disease research and threatening exile
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where the mayor treats Larry's paranoia as an actual threat to medical progress.

jokes.text: Mayor Bloomberg tells Larry to get out of New York City
jokes.setup: Larry appearing to mock Michael J. Fox at charity event
jokes.punchline: Mayor personally banishes Larry from the entire city
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Larry's mishaps reach absurd consequences.

jokes.text: Larry calls someone 'Asshole' twice at the end in Paris
jokes.setup: Larry in Paris, presumably still dealing with consequences
jokes.punchline: Still getting into conflicts even in exile
jokes.explanation: Weak ending that just shows Larry hasn't learned anything. Feels tacked on.

tmdb_overview: Larry accuses his neighbor, Michael J. Fox of harassment; Jeff takes a bullet for Susie; Larry gives an inappropriate gift to Elizabeth's son.
